title: maukschia laevis
intro: Maukschia laevis, also known as Pau'aha'aha in Hawaiian, is a rare and endangered plant species that belongs to the Gesneriaceae family. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Maukschia laevis are typically made by infusing the leaves in water or oil, and can be used topically to treat various skin conditions.
